steps.title: Track the study's finding
steps.content: The passage begins with animals known to have 3D vision and animals thought to lack it. Mercer's team tested cuttlefish with 3D images, and the cuttlefish adjusted their striking position, suggesting their vision resembles that of sheep and macaques.

steps.title: Avoid narrow or unsupported claims
steps.content: Choice A says cuttlefish eyes are more complex, which the passage never claims. Choice B misstates what was shown about sheep and macaques. Choice C focuses only on the testing method. The main idea is the evidence for cuttlefish 3D vision. The answer is D.
